sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(87) — Martis Creek from the Martis Lake dam downstream to the confluence with the Truckee River (Nevada Co.)
USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 263239, "Martis Creek", Stream, CA, Nevada/Placer Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":263239,"gaz_name":"Martis Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Nevada"/"Placer","objectid_1":1400192,"isunknowncoords":2}, mouth point (Web Mercator -13371504.1672, 4771918.1616 → 39.34991°N, 120.11827°W). Corrects prior file coordinates (-120.1490, 39.3280), which were roughly 3.6 km southwest of the actual mouth (confluence with the Truckee River) and appear to have been a guessed estimate rather than a GNIS pull; the corrected point is corroborated by the Wikipedia infobox mouth coordinate for Martis Creek, 39°21′00″N 120°07′06″W (39.35000°N, 120.11833°W).

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: (87) Martis Creek from the Martis Lake dam downstream to the confluence with the Truckee River (Nevada Co.). All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: Open all year but effectively catch-and-release only: 0 trout may be kept and only artificial lures with barbless hooks are permitted.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.
